817	\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
818	\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}
819	
820	The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
821	obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
822	rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
823	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
824	requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
825	it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
826	\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
827	\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
828	its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
829	package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
830	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
831	universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.
832	
833	\begin{definition}\leanok
834	[The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\)]
835	  \label{def:gr_bundleTransition}
836	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition}
837	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
838	    lem:gr_minorDet_unit, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition}
839	  Let \(I, J \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) be size-\(d\) subsets and let
840	  \(f_{IJ} : U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\) and \(t_{IJ} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\) be
841	  the overlap immersion and transition of the chart cover (\cref{def:gr_transition}). The
842	  \emph{bundle transition} \(g_{I,J}\) is the isomorphism of sheaves of modules on the
843	  overlap \(U^I_J\)
844	  \[
845	    g_{I,J} \;:\;
846	      f_{IJ}^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\bigr)
847	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
848	      (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^J}^{\,d}\bigr)
849	  \]
850	  induced by the invertible \(d \times d\) matrix
851	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J)\) (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
852	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor, which is invertible because
853	  \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}).
854	
855	  \emph{Realisation.} The transition is built exactly as the chart quotient
856	  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}: each entry \((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q} \in R^I_J\) is a global
857	  function on the overlap, its scalar endomorphism
858	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q}\bigr)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the
859	  \((p,q)\)-component, and the universal property of the rank-\(d\) biproduct assembles
860	  these into an automorphism of the free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); the
861	  source and target pullbacks of free sheaves are identified with this
862	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\) through the free-pullback comparisons
863	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(f_{IJ}\) and \(t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI}\), conjugating
864	  the matrix automorphism into the displayed isomorphism. Invertibility of the matrix makes
865	  \(g_{I,J}\) an isomorphism.
866	\end{definition}
867	
868	\begin{lemma}\leanok
869	[Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)]
870	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}
871	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_self}
872	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
873	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
874	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
875	  The self-transition is the identity: \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\) on
876	  \(f_{II}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\). This is the module-level form of the descent
877	  condition (C1) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\).
878	\end{lemma}
879	\begin{proof}
880	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
881	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
882	  The \(I\)-th minor of the universal matrix is the identity block, \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
883	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), so its Cramer inverse is again the
884	  identity, \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}).
885	  Hence in the biproduct presentation of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} the diagonal
886	  components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
887	  off-diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}), so
888	  the assembled automorphism is the identity of \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_I}^{\,d}\); conjugation by
889	  the free-pullback comparisons preserves the identity, giving \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\).
890	\end{proof}
891	
892	\begin{lemma}[Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap]
893	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}
894	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix}
895	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
896	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
897	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
898	  Over the common triple-overlap ring (the localisation carrying the three charts'
899	  data to \(V_{IJK}\)) the Cramer inverses of the localised minors satisfy the
900	  multiplicative cocycle identity
901	  \[
902	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}.
903	  \]
904	  This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent of any sheaf data.
905	\end{lemma}
906	\begin{proof}
907	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
908	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
909	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
910	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
911	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
912	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
913	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
914	  \[
915	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
916	  \]
917	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
918	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
919	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
920	  \[
921	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
922	  \]
923	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
924	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
925	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
926	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
927	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
928	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
929	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
930	\end{proof}
931	
932	\begin{lemma}[Transport and endpoint alignment of the bundle transitions]
933	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
934	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_transport}
935	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
936	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
937	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
938	  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) with triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\),
939	  and let \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}, \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}, \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\) be
940	  the base-change transports (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the
941	  three bundle transitions to \(V_{IJK}\). Under the free-pullback comparisons
942	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) and the endpoint bridges
943	  (\cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}), each transported transition is identified with the
944	  matrix automorphism of the corresponding base-changed Cramer inverse on the common
945	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\); granting the matrix identity
946	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}, the composite satisfies
947	  \[
948	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
949	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}
950	  \]
951	  as isomorphisms of sheaves of modules on \(V_{IJK}\). This is the substantive
952	  transport step: the alignment of the three transports' domains and codomains.
953	\end{lemma}
954	\begin{proof}
955	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
956	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
957	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
958	  Each bundle transition is, by \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, the matrix automorphism
959	  \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}\) of a localised Cramer inverse, conjugated by the
960	  free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} on its source and target.
961	  Transporting to \(V_{IJK}\) along the relevant projection or triple transition by
962	  \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} reassociates the iterated pullbacks
963	  through the pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}; the resulting
964	  source, middle, and target sheaves are pinned to a single free sheaf
965	  \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\) by the three endpoint bridges
966	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}: the \emph{src} bridge identifies the common source of
967	  \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\), the \emph{mid} bridge the
968	  target of \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) with the source of \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}\),
969	  and the \emph{tgt} bridge the target of \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}\) with that of
970	  \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\). Under these identifications the free-pullback comparisons
971	  cancel and each transported transition becomes
972	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the corresponding base-changed Cramer inverse:
973	  \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^I_J)^{-1})\),
974	  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^J_K)^{-1})\),
975	  \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^I_K)^{-1})\). By the linkage
976	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} the composite of the first two is
977	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\bigl((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\), and the matrix identity
978	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} rewrites the argument as \((X^I_K)^{-1}\), so the
979	  composite equals \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\). The bridges guarantee both sides are
980	  isomorphisms between the same pair of sheaves on \(V_{IJK}\), so the equality
981	  is well-formed.
982	\end{proof}

984	\begin{lemma}\leanok
985	[Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)]
986	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul}
987	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle}
988	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
989	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
990	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:modules_pullbackComp}
991	  Over each triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\) the base-change transports
992	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three bundle transitions satisfy
993	  \[
994	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
995	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J},
996	  \]
997	  the module-level form of the descent condition (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for
998	  the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\). This is the \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued multiplicative cocycle
999	  of \(\mathcal{U}\).
1000	\end{lemma}
1001	\begin{proof}
1002	  \uses{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1003	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1004	  The statement decomposes into three independent steps. First, the matrix-algebra core
1005	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} establishes the Cramer-inverse cocycle
1006	  \((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\) over the triple overlap. Second, the linkage
1007	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} records that composition of matrix automorphisms realises
1008	  matrix multiplication. Third, the transport step \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1009	  carries the three transitions to the common overlap \(V_{IJK}\), aligns their source,
1010	  middle, and target sheaves through the base-change transport and the endpoint bridges, and
1011	  -- feeding it the matrix identity through the linkage -- yields exactly the stated equality
1012	  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K} = \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\).
1013	\end{proof}

731	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
732	  underlying abelian-group presheaf of their relative tensor product
733	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
734	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) is naturally the coequalizer of the
735	  parallel pair
736	  \[
737	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
738	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
739	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
740	  \]
741	  of abelian-group presheaves, where the first map acts the ring factor on the left,
742	  \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto (r \cdot p) \otimes q\), and the second on the
743	  right, \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto p \otimes (r \cdot q)\). The identification
744	  is objectwise and natural in \(U\).
745	\end{lemma}
746	
747	\begin{proof}
748	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
749	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
750	    lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib,
751	    lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib,
752	    def:relTensorActL, def:relTensorActR, def:relTensorProj}
753	  The argument promotes the objectwise coequalizer of
754	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} to the functor category, in
755	  three steps.
756	
757	  \textbf{1. Objectwise.} For each open \(U\), apply
758	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} with \(S = \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
759	  \(M = \mathcal{P}(U)\), \(N = \mathcal{Q}(U)\): the abelian group
760	  \(\mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is the coequalizer
761	  in \(\mathrm{AddCommGrp}\) of the two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-action maps
762	  \[
763	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X(U)
764	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)
765	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
766	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U),
767	  \]
768	  with cofork map the projection \(\pi_U\).
769	
770	  \textbf{2. Naturality / promotion.} The three objectwise families are precisely
771	  the natural transformations of presheaves on \(X\)
772	  \[
773	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X
774	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
775	    \;\overset{a_L,\,a_R}{\rightrightarrows}\;
776	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
777	    \;\overset{\pi}{\longrightarrow}\;
778	    \bigl(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\bigr)
779	  \]
780	  built in the previous definitions: the parallel pair is the left- and
781	  right-action transformations \(a_L\) (\cref{def:relTensorActL}) and \(a_R\)
782	  (\cref{def:relTensorActR}) from the triple presheaf
783	  (\cref{def:relTensorTriplePresheaf}) to the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaf
784	  (\cref{def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}), and the cofork leg is the projection
785	  \(\pi\) (\cref{def:relTensorProj}) onto the apex. These are morphisms of
786	  functors \((\mathrm{Opens}\,X)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{Ab}\); naturality of
787	  each in \(U\) is the compatibility of the module action with the restriction
788	  maps of \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{O}_X, \mathcal{Q}\) (the restriction of
789	  \(s \cdot m\) is \((s|_V) \cdot (m|_V)\), and likewise for the projection),
790	  recorded in those definitions. Objectwise at each \(U\) the cofork
791	  \((a_L, a_R, \pi)_U\) is the colimit cofork of step 1. Colimits in a functor
792	  category are computed objectwise --- by
793	  \cref{lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib} a cocone is a colimit as
794	  soon as every evaluation \((\mathrm{ev}_U)\) of it is --- so the objectwise
795	  coequalizers of step 1 promote to a coequalizer of the displayed parallel pair
796	  \(a_L, a_R\) with cofork \(\pi\) in
797	  \((\mathrm{Opens}\,X)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{Ab}\).
798	
799	  \textbf{3. Identification of the apex.} The apex presheaf
800	  \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is, by the
801	  objectwise tensor formula of the presheaf monoidal structure
802	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}: \((\mathcal{M}_1 \otimes_p
803	  \mathcal{M}_2)(U) = \mathcal{M}_1(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}
804	  \mathcal{M}_2(U)\)), exactly the underlying abelian-group presheaf of the
805	  relative tensor product \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
806	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). Transporting the colimit of step 2
807	  along this identification exhibits
808	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\), as an abelian-group
809	  presheaf, as the coequalizer of the parallel pair, naturally in \(U\).
810	\end{proof}
811	
812	\begin{lemma}[Objectwise colimits in a functor category]
813	  \label{lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib}
814	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits}
815	  \mathlibok
816	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
817	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FunctorCategory.Basic}).}
